description: Computed values of the partial sum D_F(t; N) = Σ_{n≤N} a_n(F) / n^(1/2 + it) for different function classes

f_rand.name: Random multiplicative function
f_rand.coefficients: a_p = ±1 uniform i.i.d. at primes, extended multiplicatively

L_DH.name: Davenport-Heilbronn function
L_DH.rh_status: FAILS - has zeros off critical line

T_range.description: Covers known off-line zero at t ≈ 85.7

grid_spacing.description: Nyquist-like resolution as specified in research program

numerical_precision.description: Ensures relative error < 10^-12 for t values up to 500

scaling_analysis.description: Power-law fit M ~ N^α for growth exponent

research_question.question: Does tracking a specific peak location as it shifts with N reveal a growth exponent closer to the theoretical value (~0.31)?
research_question.theoretical_basis: σ - 1/2 where σ = 0.8085 is the real part of known off-line zero
research_question.conclusion: The observed exponents are much smaller than theory, suggesting pre-asymptotic effects dominate in the N range [10^4, 10^6]. Larger N or refined peak tracking methods are needed.

mandatory_conditions.heavy_tails: Observed - f_rand shows K=10.5 at N=10^4
mandatory_conditions.power_law_growth: Detected but α << theory, needs larger N

references: Davenport, H. and Heilbronn, H. (1936). On the zeros of certain Dirichlet series, I and II
Spira, R. (1994). Zeros of sections of the zeta function, II
Balanzario, E. P. and Sánchez-Ortiz, J. (2007). Zeros of the Davenport-Heilbronn counterexample
